name: Acid Burrower
description: Un insecte de la taille d’un cheval avec des griffes fouisseuses et du sang acide.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Fouisseur peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Earth Eruption
features.description: Faites jaillir le Fouisseur du sol. Toutes les créatures à Portée Très Proche doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité ou être renversées, les rendant Vulnérables jusqu’à leur prochaine action.

features.name: Spit Acid
features.description: Faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles devant le Fouisseur à Portée Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 2d6 dégâts physiques et doivent cocher un Emplacement d’armure sans en recevoir les bénéfices. Si elles ne peuvent pas cocher d’Emplacement d’armure, elles doivent cocher un PV supplémentaire et vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Acid Bath
features.description: Quand le Fouisseur subit des dégâts Sévères, toutes les créatures à Portée Proche sont aspergées de son sang acide, subissant 1d10 dégâts physiques. Cette éclaboussure couvre le sol à Portée Très Proche de sang, et toutes les créatures autres que le Fouisseur qui le traversent subissent 1d6 dégâts physiques.
features.trigger: Quand le fouisseur subit des dégâts sévères

name: Amphiptere
description: A sleek, snake-like reptile with feathered wings and a draconic face.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: The Amphiptere is defeated when they take any damage. For every 3 damage a PC deals to the Amphiptere, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Discouraging Bites
features.description: A PC that marks HP from an Amphiptere's "Group Attack" loses a Hope.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Amphiptere within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Animated Rug
description: A rug animated with magic.

features.name: Topple
features.description: All creatures standing on the Rug when it animates must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they fall down, making them Vulnerable until they act.

features.name: Body Wrap
features.description: When the Rug makes a successful standard attack against a target, you can mark a Stress to make them Wrapped until they succeed on a Strength Roll. While Wrapped, the target makes attack rolls with disadvantage, and damage dealt to the Rug or the target is also dealt to the other.
features.trigger: When the Rug makes a successful standard attack against a target

name: Animated Toy
description: A toy or other small object animated by an infusion of magic.

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: The Toy is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to the Toy, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Toys within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 1 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

features.name: Underfoot
features.description: When a PC makes an attack within Very Close range of a Toy, the Toy can move into Melee range and make a standard attack.
features.trigger: PC attacks within Very Close range

name: Arcanist Guard
description: A magic-wielding guard with a ward-covered coat using magic to enhance their comrades.

features.name: Tag and Tail
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success,mark a Stressto make the targetTagged. The Arcanist always knows the direction and relative distance of aTaggedtarget (within a Very Far range). The Arcanist may only have one targetTaggedat a time.

features.name: Empower Allies
features.description: Spend 2 Fearand choose up to 3 allies within Close range to become Empowered. AnEmpoweredtarget gains a+1d4 bonus to damage rolls. All instances ofEmpowered given by the Arcanist clear if the Arcanist takes Severe damage, or at the end of the scene.

features.name: Spellshield
features.description: When an ally within Close range would take damage,mark a Stressto reduce the severity by one threshold.
features.trigger: When an ally within Close range would take damage

features.name: Warded Door
features.description: When a PC investigates or tries to open a door in a building protected by the guard,spend a Fearto reveal that it has been magically warded by the Arcanist and activate a Progress Countdown (4). Until the countdown is triggered, that door cannot be opened or unlocked.
features.trigger: When a PC investigates or tries to open a door in a building protected by the guard

name: Archer Guard
description: Un grand garde portant un arc long et un carquois aux flèches empennées aux couleurs de la colonie.

features.name: Hobbling Shot
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Loin. En cas de réussite, infligez 1d12+3 dégâts physiques. Si la cible coche des PV suite à cette attaque, elle a un désavantage sur les jets d’Agilité jusqu’à ce qu’elle libère au moins 1 PV.

name: Ashen Deathweaver
description: A necromancer wearing ancient, tattered robes

features.name: Levitation
features.description: The Deathweaver levitates several feet off the ground and can’t be Restrained.

features.name: Rise, My Minions
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a Zombie Pack, which appears at Close range and immediately takes the spotlight.

features.name: Strike as One
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight a number of other allies equal to half the Deathweaver’s unmarked Stress.

features.name: Foul Judgement
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Far range. All targets within a Very Close area around that point must make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 1d10 direct magic damage and become Judged by the Deathweaver until they clear 1 HP. A Judged target loses a Hope each time they mark HP.

features.name: Arcane Barrage
features.description: Mark any number of Stress and choose a target within Close range. The target takes 1d6 magic damage per Stress marked.

features.name: Return, Selnyx! (Phase Change)
features.description: Once per scene, when the Deathweaver marks their last HP, they clear 6 HP and immediately take the spotlight to summon an Undead Wyvern, which appears at Very Close range.

name: Ashkadavar
description: A wizard's familiar, created from the bodies of several woodland creatures.

features.name: Magical Hunger
features.description: Spellcast rolls that target the Ashkadavar have disadvantage. If a PC makes a spellcast roll within Far range and fails, the Ashkadavar can mark a Stress to summon an identical Ashkadavar within Very Close range.

features.name: Unreal Existence
features.description: The Ashkadavar has resistance to physical damage. Additionally, it can mark a Stress to teleport to a location within Far range.

features.name: Magic Connection
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a Success, spend a Fear. The Ashkadavar gains a +2 bonus to its damage for each other Ashkadavar within Very Close range of the target.

name: Ashkadavar Swarm
description: A group of replicating chimeric creations made from the bodies of several woodland creatures.

features.name: Horde (1d4+2)
features.description: When the Swarm has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+2 magic damage instead.

features.name: Magical Hunger
features.description: Spellcast rolls that target the Swarm have disadvantage. If a PC makes a spellcast roll within Far range and fails, the Swarm can mark a Stress and clear 1 HP.

features.name: Magic Surge
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to gain a bonus to the damage roll equal to the number of the Swarm's unmarked HP.

name: Barrow Gaunt
description: A ghoulish undead with a semblance of intelligence and a hunger for flesh

features.name: Unsettling
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear when targeting the Barrow Gaunt with an action roll mark a Stress.

features.name: Grasping Hands
features.description: Mark a Stress to cause a target within Melee range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. The difficulty increases by 1 for each ally within Melee range of the target. On a failure, the target is Restrained and loses a Hope.

name: Bear
description: Un grand ours au pelage épais et aux griffes puissantes.

features.name: Overwhelming Force
features.description: Les cibles qui cochent des PV suite à l’attaque standard de l’Ours sont repoussées à Portée Très Proche.

features.name: Bite
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée. En cas de réussite, infligez 3d4+10 dégâts physiques et la cible est Entravée jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec un jet de Force réussi.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand l’Ours réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand l’ours réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Bladed Guard
description: Un garde en armure portant une épée et un bouclier peint aux couleurs de la colonie.

features.name: Shield Wall
features.description: Une créature qui tente de se déplacer à Portée Très Proche du Garde doit réussir un jet d’Agilité. Si d’autres Gardes à l’Épée se tiennent en ligne à ses côtés, chacun à Portée de Mêlée d’un autre garde de la ligne, la Difficulté augmente du nombre total de gardes dans cette ligne.

features.name: Detain
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, Entravez la cible jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec une attaque réussie, un jet de Finesse ou un jet de Force.

name: Blooming Cockatrice
description: A green, dragon-like creature with a moss-covered rooster's neck and bright flower petal wings.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Cockatrice gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Eyes of Leaf and Stem
features.description: Before a PC within Close range makes an action roll that would affect the Cockatrice, they must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (10). On a failure, the PC marks a Stress as they begin to turn into a tree and start an Arborization Countdown (4). It ticks down when any PC in the scene rolls with Fear. When it triggers, the target is Restrained and Unconscious; they can't make action rolls, can't speak, and automatically fail all reaction rolls. All creatures that the Cockatrice has turned into trees clear both conditions when the Cockatrice is defeated.

features.name: Penetrating Peck
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to force the target to mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they have an Arborization Countdown, tick it down by 1. If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Baleful Crow
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight 1d4+1 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way have advantage but deal half damage.

features.name: Mama Hen
features.description: When a Budding Cockatrice Clutch marks HP, you can mark a Stress to fly into Melee range of the attacker and make a "Penetrating Peck" attack against them without spending a Fear.
features.trigger: Budding Cockatrice Clutch marks HP

name: Brawny Zombie
description: Un zombie imposant doté d’une force surnaturelle.

name: Budding Cockatrice Clutch
description: A group of young dragon-like creatures with green fuzzy scales and tightly wrapped buds for wings waiting to bloom.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Clutch have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1 magic damage instead.

features.name: Thicket Sprout
features.description: When the Clutch have marked half or more of their HP, mark a Stress to create a bramble thicket around the Clutch in a Very Close range. All targets within the area must make a Finesse Reaction Roll (12). Targets who fail take 2d8 physical damage and are temporarily Restrained. Targets who succeed take half damage and are knocked out of the area. The Clutch can move through this area unimpeded and has a +3 bonus to its Difficulty while in the bramble thicket.
features.trigger: half or more HP marked

name: Burroska Doll
description: A wooden humanoid the size of a doll. They are dressed in leaves and have spiky growths all over their bodies.

features.name: Spine Explosion
features.description: When the Doll marks its last HP, all creatures within Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 1d6 physical damage.
features.trigger: marks last HP

name: Busybody
description: A meddling civilian determined to meddle with other peopleʼs business.

features.name: Practiced Manipulator
features.description: Up to three times per scene, the Busybody may utilize an experience without spending Fear.

features.name: Meddling
features.description: Spend a Fearto reveal that the Busybody has been following the current interpersonal situation as they step in to harass anyone opposing their agenda. Each PC present must make a Presence Reaction Roll. All targets that fail mark a Stress. Somebody Has to Do Something About This! – Action:Mark a Stressto have the Busybody rile up a crowd and activate a Consequence Countdown (5). When the countdown triggers, the crowd takes drastic action and you gain1d4+1Fear. The Busybody may only have one of these countdowns active at a time.

features.name: How Rude
features.description: When a target within Close range rebuffs or challenges the busybody,mark a Stressto force the target to mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When a target within Close range rebuffs or challenges the busybody

name: Cairnshade
description: A ghoulish undead thief with a long tongue and sharp teeth

features.name: Unsettling
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear when targeting the Cairnshade with an action roll mark a Stress.

features.name: Climber
features.description: The Cairnshade climbs just as easily as they run.

features.name: Dead Drop
features.description: Make an attack against a target below the Cairnshade within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes 1d10+3 physical damage and has disadvantage on their next action roll.

name: Canid Jackal
description: A jackal-like humanoid with dozens of criminal connections.

features.name: Bark Order
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight an ally within Close range with the Canid. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Go Fetch
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range to attempt to disarm them. On a success, take their primary weapon and throw it up to Close range. If the target doesn't have a secondary weapon equipped, they become Vulnerable until they rearm themselves.

features.name: Blood Bond
features.description: If an ally within Close range takes damage, the Canid can take the damage instead.
features.trigger: If an ally within Close range takes damage

features.name: Opportunist
features.description: The Jackal deals double damage to targets that are Vulnerable.

features.name: Lie Down
features.description: Mark a Stress to tell a target within Close range to drop to the floor, mimicking the voice of one of the target's allies. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or feel compelled to obey. When the target acts they become Prone until they next have the spotlight. While Prone their movement is reduced to Very Close range, they have disadvantage on attack rolls, and they are Vulnerable.

features.name: Call for Reinforcements
features.description: Summon 1d4+l Canid Rovers, who appear at Far range.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Jackal makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Centaurs Centaurs are humanoids with the lower body of a horse, who often roam forests and open plains in herds. As well as fighting prowess, centaurs are skilled stargazers from which they have divination abilities. Centaur Archer Centaur Archers are skilled hunters, deadly at range even at full gallop.
features.trigger: When the Jackal makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Canid Rover
description: A dog-like humanoid transient worker.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Canids Rovers within Close range of them. Those Minions make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

features.name: Blood Bond
features.description: If an ally within Close range takes damage, the Canid can take the damage instead.
features.trigger: If an ally within Close range takes damage

name: Caustic Fungus
description: The underbrush of the Screaming Forest has a hive mind of

features.name: Minion (4)
features.description: Caustic Fungus is defeated when they take any damage. For every 4 damage a PC deals to a Caustic Fungus, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Corrosive
features.description: Targets who are successfully hit by the Fungus’ attacks must also mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Caustic Fungus within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Cave Ogre
description: Un humanoïde massif qui considère toute vie intelligente comme de la nourriture.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: Vous devez dépenser une Peur pour placer l’Ogre sous le projecteur. Tant qu’il est sous le projecteur, il peut faire son attaque standard contre toutes les cibles à portée.

features.name: Bone Breaker
features.description: Les attaques de l’Ogre infligent des dégâts directs.

features.name: Hail of Boulders
features.description: Ramassez des objets lourds et lancez-les sur toutes les cibles devant l’Ogre à Portée Loin. Faites une attaque contre ces cibles. Les cibles touchées subissent 1d10+2 dégâts physiques. Si l’Ogre touche plus d’une cible, vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Rampaging Fury
features.description: Quand l’Ogre coche 2 PV ou plus, il peut se déchaîner. Déplacez l’Ogre vers un point à Portée Proche et infligez 2d6+3 dégâts physiques directs à toutes les cibles sur son passage.
features.trigger: Quand l’ogre coche 2 pv ou plus, il peut se déchaîner

name: Chort
description: A whispering devil cloaked in charm and malice, the Chort offers what you desire most—then watches you unravel. His bargains are binding, his flattery venom, and his grin never quite reaches his eyes.

features.name: All Eyes on Me
features.description: PCs that are Curious have disadvantage on attacks against the Chort.

features.name: Whispered Pact
features.description: Spend a Fear to tempt a PC within Close range. The PC must mark a Stress and become Curious. While Curious, they are Vulnerable and must move as close as possible to the Chort. The PC is Curious until they succeed on an attack roll with Hope against the Chort, or the Chort is defeated.

features.name: Your Friends Will Fail You
features.description: When a PC fails with Fear, you can mark a Stress to cause all other PCs within Close range to lose a Hope.

name: Clank Petard
description: A humanoid clank with a compartment containing explosives. Motives & Tactics: Infiltrate, set charges, detonate

features.name: Explosive
features.description: Countdown (3). Once per scene, place an explosive and activate the countdown. The countdown can be ended with a successful Finesse Roll (14) while in Melee range of the explosive. When it triggers, all creatures within Very Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d4+10 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Infiltrator
features.description: When the Clank makes a successful attack, you can mark a Stress to become Hidden until after the Clank's next action or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them. The roll is made with advantage if the Clank has any HP marked due to loose parts exposing their location.
features.trigger: When the Clank makes a successful attack

features.name: Dead Man's Switch
features.description: When the Clank marks their last HP and hasn't used their Explosive feature, activate the Explosive feature with a countdown of 1. Cult ofBlood Blood Cleric Cultists work towards a common goal such as gaining power, immortality or ascension for their leader, summoning or freeing a worshipped entity, or seeking hidden or forbidden knowledge. Here, stat blocks are included for the Cult of Blood, who use blood magic rituals to attain their goals. Bloodborn When a creature is sacrificed in a powerful blood ritual, the sacrificed being rises again as a Bloodborn.
features.trigger: When the Clank marks their last HP and hasn't used their Explosive feature

name: Construct
description: Un être grossièrement humanoïde de pierre et d’acier, assemblé et animé par la magie.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Le Constructe peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à deux fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Weak Structure
features.description: Quand le Constructe coche des PV suite à des dégâts physiques, il doit cocher un PV supplémentaire.

features.name: Trample
features.description: Faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles sur le passage du Constructe quand il se déplace. Les cibles touchées subissent 1d8 dégâts physiques.

features.name: Overload
features.description: Avant de lancer les dégâts de l’attaque du Constructe, gagnez un bonus de +10 au jet de dégâts. Le Constructe peut ensuite reprendre le projecteur.

features.name: Death Quake
features.description: Quand le Constructe coche son dernier PV, la magie qui l’anime se rompt en une explosion de force. Faites une attaque avec avantage contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche.
features.trigger: Quand le constructe coche son dernier pv

name: Courtier
description: Un mondain ambitieux et ostensiblement vêtu.

features.name: Mockery
features.description: Dites quelque chose de moqueur et forcez une cible à Portée Proche à faire un jet de Réaction de Présence (14). En cas d’échec, la cible doit cocher 2 Stress et est Vulnérable jusqu’à la fin de la scène.

features.name: Scapegoat
features.description: Ciblez un PJ. Le Courtisan convainc une foule ou un individu éminent que la cible est la cause de leur conflit ou malheur actuel.

name: Cryptwight
description: A rotted corpse weaving terror with its shadowy spells

features.name: Unsettling
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear when targeting the Cryptwight with an action roll mark a Stress.

features.name: Rotting Touch
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 1d6 magic direct damage and the Cryptwight clears 1 HP.

features.name: Death Sign
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes no damage, but becomes gripped with painful memories of loss, losing a Hope and marking a Stress.

features.name: Graven Claws
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose a point within Far range. A Close area around that point erupts with clawed hands of the dead. All targets within or who enter the area must make a Strength Reaction Roll (13). Targets who fail are Restrained.

name: Deeproot Defender
description: Une personne-végétale robuste aux lianes agrippantes.

features.name: Ground Slam
features.description: Frappez le sol, repoussant toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche jusqu’à Portée Loin. Chaque cible repoussée doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Grab and Drag
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Proche. En cas de réussite, tirez-la à Portée de Mêlée, infligez 1d6+2 dégâts physiques et Entravez-la jusqu’à ce que le Défenseur subisse des dégâts Sévères.

name: Dire Wolf
description: Un grand loup aux crocs menaçants, rarement rencontré seul.

features.name: Pack Tactics
features.description: Si le Loup réussit une attaque standard et qu’un autre Loup Sinistre est à Portée de Mêlée de la cible, infligez 1d6+5 dégâts physiques au lieu des dégâts standard et vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Hobbling Strike
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée. En cas de réussite, infligez 3d4+10 dégâts physiques directs et rendez-la Vulnérable jusqu’à ce qu’elle libère au moins 1 PV.

name: Dolliglop
description: A small, ooze-like creature that inhabits a child's doll or other toy and feeds off emotion.

features.name: Pay the Price
features.description: When a PC spends Hope within Far range of the Dolliglop, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Island of Misfits
features.description: Twice per scene, spend a Fear to summon a number of Animated Toys equal to twice the number of PCs. They appear within Far range and make a "Group Attack" against a chosen target.

features.name: Outgrown and Discarded
features.description: Spend a Fear to emanate a wave of loss and sadness from the Dolliglop. Each target within Far range must make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target vividly recalls a memory of a lost toy or keepsake and they have disadvantage on all action rolls until they roll with Hope. Targets who succeed mark a Stress.

features.name: Feed on Hope
features.description: If a PC rolls with Hope within Far range of the Dolliglop, you can mark a Stress and roll a d20. If the result is equal to or lower than the number on the PC's Hope die, the Dolliglop clears 1 HP and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: PC rolls with Hope within Far range

name: Drakona Berserker
description: A scarred drakona who fights for money.

features.name: Reckless
features.description: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on a standard attack.

features.name: Adrenaline Burst
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to clear 2 HP and 2 Stress.

features.name: Elemental Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range in front of the Drakona. On a success, deal 1d8 magic damage. The Drakona of Cindermount have built a giant golden I dragon for their leader toplace his soul into andawaken. - Archibald Drakona Elementalist Elementalists are sorcerers who channel the power of the elements. Drakona Monk Monks use martial arts to channel and strike with focused elemental power.

name: Drekavac
description: A twisted echo of an unbaptized soul, the Drekavac haunts graveyards and dark woods, its piercing scream shattering sleep, sanity, and spirit. It is both a curse left behind and a warning yet to come.

features.name: Horrifying
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Drekavac's attacks must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Wail of the Forgotten
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Close range. On a success, deal 2d6+3 magical damage and make them Vulnerable until they mark 2 Stress.

features.name: Feed on Terror
features.description: When the Drekavac succeeds on an attack against a target that is Vulnerable, they can mark a Stress to change that damage to direct magical damage.

name: Eryn (Limb Wreath)
description: Eryn has become something of nightmares, a host for

features.name: Horrifying
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Limb Wreath’s attacks must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Barrage of Tendrils
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack roll against up to 3 targets within Close range. Any you succeed against take1d8+2physical damage and must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll (13) or be pulled in Melee range of the Limb Wreath.

features.name: Taste for Blood
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes2d8physical damage, and the Limb Wreath clears 2 HP.

features.name: Living Tentacles
features.description: When the Limb Wreath marks 2 or more HP from an attack, you can mark a Stress to create a Living Tentacle within Very Close range. The Tentacle is immediately spotlighted.

name: Faerie Socialite
description: A trendy faerie with an appearance matching the season.

features.name: Probing Gossip
features.description: Describe how the Faerie has heard a rumor about a PC. The PC must succeed on a Presence Roll or mark 2 Stress.

features.name: Seasonal Burst
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a seasonal effect that extends out to Close range and lasts until the Faerie marks HP. Targets within the affected area have disadvantage on an action roll. The effect depends on the season: a pollen cloud that causes sneezing (spring), a whorl of fire that produces smoke (summer), darkness that causes melancholy (fall), or icy vapor that creates freezing mists (winter).

name: Forlorn Spirit
description: A distraught or displaced soul, seeking either redemption or solace.

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Spirit has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Forlorn Wail
features.description: Spend a Fear to howl at all targets within Far range, forcing them to make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target must use a d10 for their Hope die until they roll with Hope or the scene ends. On a success, the target marks a Stress, but gleans a course of action that might put the Spirit to rest.

features.name: Feel Your Pain
features.description: When a PC marks HP within Close range of the Spirit, mark a Stress to have the Spirit immediately move into Melee range of that PC and make a standard attack.
features.trigger: PC marks HP within Close range

name: Fungril Gladeservant
description: A towering fungril dressed in what nature provides with a wooden blade arm and a network of vines which serve as fingers.

features.name: Entranced By Song
features.description: When a PC within Close range sings a lullaby they can make a Presence Roll (15) to cause the Gladeservant to fall Asleep until the Gladeservant marks HP or the GM spends a Fear to awaken them. While Asleep, the Gladeservant cannot act.

features.name: Blade Swing
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Gladeservant. Targets the Gladeservant succeeds against take 1d8+3 physical damage and mark a Stress.

features.name: Grasping Vine
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target within Close range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the Gladeservant Envelops them and the target must mark 2 Stress. While Enveloped, the target is Restrained and must mark an additional Stress every time they make an action roll. When the Gladeservant takes Severe damage, falls Asleep, or is defeated, all Enveloped targets are freed and the condition is cleared.

features.name: Stone's Throw
features.description: Mark a Stress to throw a heavy object at a point within Far range. All targets within a Very Close range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d6 physical damage.

features.name: Rage of the Trees
features.description: When the Gladeservant takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can spend a Fear to make a standard attack against the attacker at advantage. On a success, clear a Stress.
features.trigger: takes damage from attack within Close range

name: Gang Hideout
description: A gathering point used by a gang as a base of operations and to shift goods, often attached to a legitimate business.

features.name: Illegal Enterprise
features.description: The illicit activities the gang is involved with are set up in the basement or back room of a legitimate business. A special pass phrase gains access to restricted areas. What is the illegal operation - drugs, smuggling, blood sports, or something else?

features.name: Gambling
features.description: A gambling game such as betting on a Giant Rat fight is in progress. A PC can place a bet on an outcome. Roll a d2O to determine the outcome. If the odds are equal, the PC wins double their bet on a roll of 11 or higher. If the PC bets on the favorite, they win their bet plus 50% on a roll of 6 or higher. If they bet on the underdog, they win quadruple their bet on a roll of 16 or higher. What do the PCs gamble on? Are the real odds different from the presented odds?

features.name: Guard Raid
features.description: Spend a Fear to raid the hideout with a Head Guard, two Bladed Guards, and two Archer Guards. They take on NPC threats in addition to the PCs. How do the PCs feel being on the wrong side of the law? Are there Secret means of escape?

features.name: We're Actually the Good Guys
features.description: If the PCs put themselves at odds with the Gang, one of the adversaries in the room immediately takes the spotlight and makes an attack. How many adversaries are in the room? Will reinforcements join them?
features.trigger: If the PCs put themselves at odds with the Gang

name: Ghast Hollowheart
description: A multi-limbed corpse with a cruel flail and spines that thrust from its body

features.name: Unsettling
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear when targeting the Hollowheart with an action roll mark a Stress.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Hollowheart can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Aura of Pestilence
features.description: A PC who tries to move within Very Close range of the Hollowheart must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or mark a Stress.

features.name: Savage Sweep
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Hollowerheart succeeds against take 2d6+3 physical damage and mark a Stress. If the attack is a critical success, targets mark 3 Stress.

features.name: Cruel Intentions
features.description: If a PC attacks the Hollowheart from Far or greater range, Spend a Fear to move into Very Close distance of the attacker and force them to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a Failure, the attacker is Restrained. They can free themselves with a successful Strength Roll (14). While Restrained, the target can only attack the Hollowheart.
features.trigger: If a PC attacks the Hollowheart from Far or greater range

name: Ghost
description: A restless spirited soul who haunts an area, driven by one final desire.

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Ghost is resistant to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Possess
features.description: Mark a Stress to attempt to take control of a PC's body within Melee range. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Possessed by the Ghost. While Possessed, they can't act until they succeed on an Instinct Roll to end the possession. Attacks made against the Possessed target damage the target, after which they can attempt a new Instinct Reaction Roll to end the possession. Spotlight the Possessed target instead of the Ghost. Roll a d20 instead of Duality Dice to make an attack, and if you use an ability that costs Hope, spend Fear instead.

name: Ghoul Pack
description: A pack of long limbed, flesh-eating undead, dripping in blood and gore

features.name: Horde (d+)
features.description: When the Zombies have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack dealsd+physical damage instead.

features.name: Feast
features.description: If the Ghoul Pack has any marked HP, spend a Fear to feast on a corpse and clear a HP

name: Giant Mosquitoes
description: Des dizaines de moustiques gros comme le poing, volant ensemble pour se protéger.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: Quand les Moustiques ont coché la moitié ou plus de leurs PV, leur attaque standard inflige 1d4+1 dégâts physiques à la place.

features.name: Flying
features.description: En vol, les Moustiques ont un bonus de +2 à leur Difficulté.

features.name: Bloodsucker
features.description: Quand l’attaque des Moustiques fait cocher des PV à une cible, forcez la cible à cocher un PV supplémentaire.
features.trigger: Quand l’attaque des moustiques fait cocher des pv à une cible

name: Giant Rat
description: Un rongeur de la taille d’un chat, doué pour fouiller et survivre.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: Le Rat est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 3 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige au Rat, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Rats Géants à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 1 dégât physique chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Giant Scorpion
description: Un arachnide de taille humaine avec des pinces déchirantes et une queue venimeuse.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre deux cibles à Portée de Mêlée.

features.name: Venomous Stinger
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, infligez 1d4+4 dégâts physiques et Empoisonnez-la jusqu’à son prochain repos ou un jet de Savoir réussi (16). Tant qu’Empoisonnée, la cible doit lancer un d6 avant chaque jet d’action. Sur un résultat de 4 ou moins, elle doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Scorpion réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le scorpion réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Giant Snake
description: A snake large enough to swallow a small humanoid.

features.name: Constrict
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 1d6+5 direct physical damage and the target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become temporarily Restrained by the Snake. You can mark a Stress to deal this damage each time the snake acts.

features.name: Envenom
features.description: When the Snake makes a successful standard attack, you can spend a Fear to Poison the target until their next rest or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (14). While Poisoned, the target must roll a d6 before they make an action roll. On a result of 4 or lower, they must mark a Stress. Giant Tortoise Gorilla A Giant Tortoise can grow as tall as a building. Some are raised in societies of Galapa and can communicate with them. On occasion a Giant Tortoise is used as a beast of war with fortifications and a giant cannon built upon their back.
features.trigger: When the Snake makes a successful standard attack

name: Giant Toad
description: A large toad with a mouth big enough to swallow a person whole.

features.name: Leaping Crush
features.description: Mark a Stress to leap to a point within Close range. All targets within Melee range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take IdlO physical damage.

features.name: Swallow
features.description: When the Toad deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack, you can spend a Fear to swallow the target. While swallowed, the creature is Restrained within the Toad and must mark a Stress when they act. If the Toad takes Major or greater damage, all swallowed creatures are released, taking 1d6 physical damage and moving to Melee range of the Toad.
features.trigger: When the Toad deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack

name: Glass Snake
description: Un serpent transparent à la tête massive qui laisse une traînée d’éclats de verre partout où il passe.

features.name: Armor-Shredding Shards
features.description: Après une attaque réussie contre le Serpent à Portée de Mêlée, l’attaquant doit cocher un Emplacement d’armure. S’il ne peut pas, il doit cocher un PV.

features.name: Spinning Serpent
features.description: Faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 1d6+1 dégâts physiques.

features.name: Spitter
features.description: Introduisez un d6 Dé de Crachat. Quand le Serpent est sous le projecteur, lancez ce dé. Sur un résultat de 5 ou plus, toutes les cibles devant le Serpent à Portée Loin doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité ou subir 1d4 dégâts physiques. Le Serpent peut reprendre le projecteur ce tour.

name: Goblin Archer
description: A small goblinoid with large ears and big mouth.

features.name: Taunt
features.description: When a PC misses on an attack against the Goblin, they must mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC misses on an attack against the Goblin

name: Goblin Mage
description: A goblinoid with glowing skin granting magical powers.

features.name: Wild Magic Skin
features.description: Attacks that deal magic damage to the Goblin trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book).

features.name: Twin Bolts
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against two targets within Far range. Each target that you succeed against takes 1d6 magic damage. Combine the damage if both bolts strike the same target.

name: Grand Feast
description: A community-wide celebration featuring way too much drink, food, and fun.

features.name: Loose Lips and Fast Friends
features.description: Everyone is here to have a good time. PCs have advantage on Presence rolls to socialize with other revelers.

features.name: Are You Not Entertained?
features.description: Spend the spotlight to have the host of the feast kick off the entertainment portion of the evening. All PCs can make an action roll to initiate a performance, competition, or other lighthearted diversion. Each PC who succeeds becomes an Honored Guest. Until the grand feast ends, an Honored Guest can claim one boon from the host, then clear the condition.

features.name: There's Always That One...
features.description: Spend a Fear to Trap a PC in conversation with the most unpleasant person at the party until they succeed on an action roll to extricate themself from this social black hole. A Trapped PC can't make any other action rolls except to try and remove this condition. When a Trapped PC fails an action roll, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Last Call
features.description: After a long night, the partygoers start filtering out. When the feast concludes, all PCs who participated in the festivities clear all Stress and gain a Hope. Additionally, each Honored Guest clears all HP and gains an additional Hope, then clears the condition. PCs who overindulged must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Hungover until their next rest. Hungover PCs take a -1 penalty to all action rolls.
features.trigger: When the feast concludes

name: Gravescourge
description: A giant’s body rotted from the inside, holding a cleaver.

features.name: Unsettling
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear when targeting the Gravescourge with an action roll mark a Stress.

features.name: Easy Pickings
features.description: The Gravescourge has advantage against Restrained targets.

features.name: Guillotine Slam
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets in a line in front of the Gravescourge up to Close range. Targets the Gravescourge succeeds against, take 2d6+6 physical damage and are knocked away from the line to a point within Very Close range.

name: Green Ooze
description: Un blob vert translucide d’acide.

name: Grinning Legion Mob
description: An unruly mob of nihilistic ruffians from smaller- statured ancestries – goblin, halfling, goblin, and more.

features.name: Horde (1d4+1)
features.description: When the Mob has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d4+1physical damage instead.

features.name: Make a Ruckus
features.description: Mark a Stressto have the Mob yell and jeer in a cacophony, which unnerves all nearby. All targets within Close must make an Instinct Reaction Roll or mark a Stress.

name: Grinning Wolfrider
description: A Grinning Legion warrior with a bird-skull mask wielding a short spear and riding a cackling wolf.

features.name: Mounted Duo
features.description: The Wolfrider can move up to a Far range as part of their action. If they move at least a Close range before making an attack, they gain advantage on the attack.

features.name: Menacing Howl
features.description: Spend a Fearto force all targets within Close range to make a Presence Reaction Roll or lose a Hope.

features.name: Run Down
features.description: When a target moves from within Very Close range,mark a Stressto move into Melee range with them and make an attack. On a success, they take an additional1d6damage and becomeVulnerableuntil they next take the spotlight. Grinning Wolfrider
features.trigger: When a target moves from within Very Close range

name: Harrier
description: Un combattant agile armé de javelots.

features.name: Maintain Distance
features.description: Après une attaque standard, le Harceleur peut se déplacer n’importe où à Portée Loin.

features.name: Fall Back
features.description: Quand une créature se déplace à Portée de Mêlée pour attaquer, avant le jet d’attaque, déplacez-vous n’importe où à Portée Proche et faites une attaque contre cette créature. En cas de réussite, infligez 1d10+2 dégâts physiques.
features.trigger: Quand une créature se déplace à portée de mêlée pour attaquer, avant le jet d’attaque

name: Head Guard
description: Un garde expérimenté avec une masse, un sifflet et une voix tonitruante.

features.name: Rally Guards
features.description: Placez sous le projecteur le Chef des Gardes et jusqu’à 2d4 alliés à Portée Loin.

features.name: On My Signal
features.description: Compte à rebours (5). Quand le Chef des Gardes est sous le projecteur pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours. Il décompte quand un PJ fait un jet d’attaque. Quand il se déclenche, tous les Gardes Archers à Portée Loin font une attaque standard avec avantage contre la cible la plus proche à leur portée. Si des attaques touchent la même cible, combinez leurs dégâts.
features.trigger: Quand le chef des gardes est sous le projecteur pour la première fois

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Chef des Gardes réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le chef des gardes réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Hedge Wizard
description: A mystic who has learned the ways of magic by learning the ways of life.

features.name: Dubious Dealings
features.description: The Hedge Wizard must be offered a gift such as a fine wine or a magic trinket before speaking openly with the PCs. PCs that do not extend this hospitality have disadvantage on all social actions made against the Hedge Wizard.

features.name: Hex
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a PC to make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, the PC must speak the truth until the end of the scene. If they knowingly tell a lie, they must mark 3 Stress.

features.name: Whispering Wanderers
features.description: If a PC threatens the Hedge Wizard with violence, spend a Fear to summon 1 Sylvan Soldier (or similar Tier 1 Standard adversary) for every 2 PCs, who appear at Close range and are immediately spotlighted.
features.trigger: PC threatens with violence

name: Hippogriff
description: A flying creature with the forelegs and head of an eagle and the hindquarters of a horse trained to accept riders.

features.name: Mount
features.description: A creature that is riding the Hippogriff can cause the Hippogriff to mark a Stress to move to a Far range when they act.

features.name: Protective
features.description: A creature riding the Hippogriff can mark a Stress to add a +2 to their Difficulty (Or evasion if a PC) when targeted by an attack. If the attack would still be successful, the Hippogriff takes the damage instead.
features.trigger: rider targeted by attack

name: Hive Collector
description: Seekers for Mother, they collect what might please her.

features.name: Sleeping Sting
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, they take1d8+3physical damage and must make a Instinct Reaction Roll (13). On a failure, they become Asleep until they take damage, or until1d4hours have passed. WhileAsleep, a PC cannot be spotlighted.

features.name: Tangle Net
features.description: Mark a Stressto make a target within Close range roll an Agility Reaction Roll (13). On a failure, they are Restrainedand Vulnerableuntil they succeed on a Strength or Agility Action Roll (15).

name: Hobblin
description: A tall fur-covered goblinoid with long muscular arms.

features.name: Ambusher
features.description: The Hobblin has advantage on attacks against targets that have not been spotlighted.

features.name: Hobbling Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 1d8 + 10 physical damage and the target is temporarily Hobbled. While Hobbled, the target can only move up to Very Close range.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Hobblin makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Hobblin makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Hobgoblin Chief
description: A tall goblinoid with an intense demeanor.

features.name: Tactician
features.description: When you spotlight the Hobgoblin, mark a Stress to also spotlight two allies within Close range.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to force an attacker to reroll an attack against the Hobgoblin.

features.name: Practiced Maneuver
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, spend a Fear to grant allies spotlighted this GM turn advantage against that target.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Hobgoblin makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Hobgoblin makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Hobgoblin Raider
description: A large goblinoid riding a giant boar that makes an ill-tempered mount.

features.name: Mounted
features.description: While the Hobgoblin is on a mount they have a +2 bonus to their Difficulty. When they take Severe damage, they're knocked from their mount and lose this benefit until they're next spotlighted.

features.name: Unruly Mount
features.description: When the Hobgoblin is spotlighted, roll a d6. On a 1 or 2, the mount ignores the Hobgoblin's intentions and immediately uses their "Charge" action without expending a Stress.

features.name: Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to move up to Far range and make an attack against a target with advantage. On a success, the target takes 2d6 physical damage and must mark a Stress.

name: Human Student
description: An apprentice mage with raw but unrefined talent.

features.name: Untrained
features.description: When the Student succeeds on an attack, roll a d6. On a 5 or 6, they trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book).

features.name: Shocking Grasp
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal IdlO magic damage and the target has disadvantage on their next action roll. Hybrid Beasts Hybrid Beasts are unnatural magical creations combining two or more types of beast. Bearcupine A Bearcupine is half-bear and half-porcupine. They can pull off spines from their body and hurl them like javelins to attack distant foes.

name: Jagged Knife Bandit
description: Un criminel rusé en cape portant l’un des couteaux emblématiques du gang.

features.name: Climber
features.description: Le Bandit grimpe aussi facilement qu’il court.

features.name: From Above
features.description: Quand le Bandit réussit une attaque standard depuis une position surélevée, il inflige 1d10+1 dégâts physiques au lieu de ses dégâts standard.

name: Jagged Knife Hexer
description: Un bandit portant un bâton et une cape ornée d’accessoires magiques, utilisant des malédictions pour tourmenter ses ennemis.

features.name: Curse
features.description: Choisissez une cible à Portée Loin et Maudissez-la temporairement. Tant que la cible est Maudite, vous pouvez cocher un Stress quand cette cible obtient Espoir pour transformer le jet en jet avec Peur à la place.

features.name: Chaotic Flux
features.description: Faites une attaque contre jusqu’à trois cibles à Portée Très Proche. Cochez un Stress pour infliger 2d6+3 dégâts magiques aux cibles touchées.

name: Jagged Knife Kneebreaker
description: Un bagarreur imposant portant un gros gourdin.

features.name: I've Got 'Em
features.description: Les créatures Entravées par le Briseur de Genoux subissent le double de dégâts des attaques des autres adversaires.

features.name: Hold Them Down
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée. En cas de réussite, la cible ne subit pas de dégâts mais est Entravée et Vulnérable. La cible peut se libérer, retirant les deux conditions, avec un jet de Force réussi, ou est libérée automatiquement si le Briseur de Genoux subit des dégâts Majeurs ou supérieurs.

name: Jagged Knife Lackey
description: Un voleur en vêtements simples avec de petites dagues, désireux de faire ses preuves.

features.name: Minion (3)
features.description: Le Larbin est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 3 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige au Larbin, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Larbins à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 2 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Jagged Knife Lieutenant
description: Un bandit aguerri en cuir de qualité, à la voix forte et au regard rusé.

features.name: Tactician
features.description: Quand vous placez le Lieutenant sous le projecteur, cochez un Stress pour aussi placer sous le projecteur deux alliés à Portée Proche.

features.name: More Where That Came From
features.description: Invoquez trois Larbins du Couteau Dentelé, qui apparaissent à Portée Loin.

features.name: Coup de Grace
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible Vulnérable à Portée Proche. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d6+12 dégâts physiques et la cible doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Lieutenant réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le lieutenant réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Jagged Knife Shadow
description: Un vaurien agile portant un couteau vicieux et utilisant la magie des ombres pour isoler ses cibles.

features.name: Backstab
features.description: Quand l’Ombre réussit une attaque standard avec avantage, elle inflige 1d6+6 dégâts physiques au lieu de ses dégâts standard.

features.name: Cloaked
features.description: Devenez Caché jusqu’après la prochaine attaque de l’Ombre. Les attaques effectuées en étant Caché grâce à cette feature ont l’avantage.

name: Jagged Knife Sniper
description: Un bandit dégingandé frappant depuis une position dissimulée avec un arc court.

features.name: Unseen Strike
features.description: Si le Tireur est Caché quand il réussit une attaque standard contre une cible, il inflige 1d10+4 dégâts physiques au lieu de ses dégâts standard.

name: Katari Witch
description: A female katari wearing a pointy hat with ear holes.

features.name: Magic Charms
features.description: The Witch is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Cat's Cradle
features.description: Mark a Stress to force all targets within Very Close range to make a Finesse Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are Restrained by magical thread until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll.

features.name: Hex
features.description: When the Witch makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Hex the target until they roll a success with Hope or the Witch is defeated. While Hexed, the target's Evasion is halved. Kobolds Kobolds are cowardly and diminutive draconic humanoids. They prefer to use sneaky methods to get the upper hand in a fight. Kobold Slinger Kobold Slingers shake up and fling hornet nests at their targets to give them a different enemy to fight. Kobold Trapper Kobold Trappers build traps on the battlefield in advance for their targets to walk into. The Kobolds trapped me in a pit with spikes and acid. I hadto explain to them that using both didn’t make sense. -	Archibald
features.trigger: When the Witch makes a successful standard attack

name: Kobold Slinger
description: A small and cowardly draconic humanoid armed with a sling.

features.name: Hornet Nest
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal damage and summon a Swarm of Hornets next to the target (reflavor Giant Mosquitoes (SRD), with 4 HP and the "Bloodsucker" reaction renamed to Stinger). Immediately spotlight the Hornets.

name: Kobold Trapper
description: A small and cowardly draconic humanoid armed with a dagger.

features.name: Pit Trap
features.description: Mark a Stress to reveal a pit trap set anywhere on the battlefield by the Kobold. All targets within Very Close range of the trap must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (13) or fall into the pit. A target takes 1d8 direct physical damage and becomes Restrained until they climb out with a successful Strength or Agility Roll (13).

features.name: Fishing For Treasure
features.description: Make an attack against a target caught in a trap. On a success, steal one item or consumable from the target's inventory. L Leviathan The Leviathan is a legendary sea beast that attacks even the largest ships. The Leviathan has three sections visible above the water line, corresponding to its head, body, and tail.

name: Leafcutter Spriggant
description: An insect the size of a large dog with wooden armor plates taken from the bark of various trees.

features.name: Acidic Bite
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to do 2d6+2 physical damage and the target must mark an Armor Slot without gaining its benefit (they can still use armor to reduce the damage).

features.name: Bark Block
features.description: When the Spriggant takes physical damage, mark a Stress to reduce it by 3.
features.trigger: takes physical damage

name: Living Tentacle
description: A part of the Limb Wreath is severed, twitching and moving

features.name: Horrifying
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Tentacle’s attacks must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Minion (5)
features.description: The Tentacle is defeated when they take any damage. For every 5 damage a PC deals to a Tentacle, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Tentacles within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 2 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Lone Spectre
description: A ghostly entity of pure malice

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Spectre has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Fearsome
features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack the target loses a Hope. When a PC fails with fear within Close range of the Spectre, you gain an extra fear. TIER TWO UNDEAD ADVERSARIES You can throw these at your Level-PCs, or your Level One PCs if you’re mean! TIER TWO Whereas Tier One is only level One, rememebr Tier Two is levels -. So Encounters might be a bit more difficult or easy than expected.My Advice - Start easy, then use Fear to have extra adversaries appear! BANSHEES A banshee, rooted in Irish and Scottish mythology as a “woman of the fairy mound,” is a female spirit whose mournful wail foretells the death of a family member. Originally a non- malevolent figure, often associated with specific noble families, the banshee’s cry was a sorrowful warning, a supernatural keening that gave the living time to prepare for the inevitable. In modern fantasy, however, the banshee has evolved into a more dangerous, undead creature. For your purposes a banshee is typically an incorporeal foe with a

features.name: signature ability
features.description: a devastating scream or “wail” that can cause damage, paralysis, or even instant death to those who hear it. This transformation from an omen of death to an active agent of destruction makes the banshee a fearsome and memorable enemy. Banshees are often encountered in haunted ruins or graveyards. BANSHEE TACTICS A Banshee can be used as a boss with a powerful, area-of-effect attack. A group will likely hear a quieter wail in the distance as they approach the Banshee who will initially appear forlorn but harmless and only turn on the party if they further upset her. GHASTS A ghast is a more powerful and dangerous variant of the ghoul. While both are undead creatures with an insatiable hunger for flesh, ghasts are distinguished by their greater intelligence and strength. Ghasts are usually bigger than Ghouls and may have more animalistic features. They often serve as leaders of ghoul packs, directing their lesser kin in raids on graveyards and unsuspecting travelers. A ghast’s most terrifying ability is its foul, necrotic stench, which can poison and sicken any living creature that comes near it. This, combined with a paralyzing touch, makes a ghast a formidable foe for even experienced adventurers, as its presence alone can weaken an entire party, leaving them vulnerable to the ghastly creature and its ravenous followers. GHAST TACTICS A Ghast should be leading Ghouls and can swarm any targets they come across.  Chapter Two: Adversaries Tier Two MUMMIES A mummy is an undead creature created through a powerful ritual to preserve the body and soul of an important individual, often a pharaoh, priest, or other figure of high status. Unlike mindless shambling zombies, mummies are often infused with a powerful and malevolent magical curse, granting them a semblance of their former intelligence and powers. They are most commonly found guarding their ancient tombs, which are often filled with traps, puzzles, and other undead servants. Their power stems from the magical preservation of their bodies, making them highly resistant to physical attacks, and they are typically able to inflict curses or diseases upon those who disturb their rest. The most dangerous mummies, known as mummy lords, are powerful spellcasters and tactical leaders who can command legions of lesser undead. At Tier Two, Mummies are more likely to be the servants of a Lord and lack the intelligence of higher Tier Mummies. MUMMY TACTICS At this tier, Mummies are pretty mindless. Shambling mummies are minions, so are useful for working as a tar pit, holding up the Heroes. Rotting Mummies can deliver a rotting touch which will cause heroes to accumulate stress which will quickly become a problem. VAMPIRES Vampires were covered in the Core Rulebook with the Vampire and Head Vampire but here I introduce a Tier Two Vampire Thrall. Vampires are highly intelligent Undead that drink the blood of living creatures. Vampires tend to form their own societies and hide in plain sight, keeping access to their food without giving their nature away. Some Vampires will be more predatory. Vampire Pups are recently turned Vampires that are servants to their masters but over time will progress to the power level of a full Vampire. VAMPIRE TACTICS Vampire Pups can be used at higher tiers to assist a Head Vampire or Vampire. At Tier Two, Vampire Pups will likely hunt in packs. WIGHTS A wight is a form of intelligent, corporeal undead, often the reanimated corpse of a warrior, king, or other powerful individual. Unlike a mindless zombie, a wight retains a semblance of its former personality and skills, making it a cunning and formidable foe. They are often found guarding the very tombs and ancient burial sites they were laid to rest in, bound by a powerful curse or an act of necromancy. A wight’s most feared ability is its “life drain”, a chilling touch that not only inflicts damage but also permanently saps the life force from its victims, sometimes even turning those it kills into its own zombie servants. They are a significant step up from standard undead, requiring players to not only overcome their martial prowess but also to find a way to counter their malevolent, life-sapping attacks. WIGHT TACTICS Wights are normally guardians, protecting tombs and other sites. They could also be used as leaders for Tier One skeletons and Zombies. Wights have intelligence and should fight as such, attacking the weaker targets or healers. WRAITHS A wraith is a powerful and malevolent incorporeal undead creature, a being of pure malice and negative energy. Unlike a simple ghost or spectre, which might be a lingering echo of a past life, a wraith is a sentient entity driven by an intense hatred for all living things. Often appearing as a shadowy, humanoid figure with glowing eyes, it is a physical manifestation of a soul so suffused with evil that it has become a void, a chilling hole in reality that devours the life force of its victims. Wraiths are known for their ability to drain the energy and strength from those they touch, and a victim who succumbs to a wraith’s attack may even rise as a subservient, lesser undead spirit. Their very presence withers plants, sickens animals, and extinguishes light, making them a tangible plague upon the land and a formidable threat for any adventurer. WRAITH TACTICS Wraiths will prey on weaker creatures to create spectres before attackign stronger foes.

On a failure, the Hound steals their shadow and the target becomesShadowless. While Shadowless, a creature isVulnerable. A creature clearsShadowlessif the Hound suffers Major damage, is defeated, or when the target has their next rest.

features.name: Play Keep-away
features.description: When a PC moves into melee range with the Hound, they canmark a Stressto move up to a Close range. Umbral Hound
features.trigger: When a PC moves into melee range with the Hound

name: Umbral Howler
description: A loud canine skeleton draped in shadow, with a howl that shatters the spirit.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: The Howler may move from one shadow to another within Close range as if they were adjacent.

features.name: Ear-splitting Voice
features.description: Mark a Stressto make a Heartbreaking Howl attack against all targets within Close range. Each target the Howler succeeds against must mark a Stress.

name: Umbral Huntmaster
description: A living shadow commanding shadowy canines like a hunting pack.

features.name: Umbral Form
features.description: The Huntmaster is Vulnerablewhile exposed to a source of extremely bright light (daylight or a large magical light).

features.name: Pack Leader
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto spotlight the Huntmaster and up to2d4allies with Far range.

features.name: Loyal Pack
features.description: When the Huntmaster would take damage,mark a Stressto make an ally within Very Close take the damage instead.
features.trigger: When the Huntmaster would take damage

features.name: Shadow Trophy
features.description: When an ally with a shadow token moves to within Melee range of the Huntmaster, the Huntmaster maymark a Stressto consume the shadow. TheShadowlesscharacter loses a Hope and you gain 2 Fear. The target can only clearShadowlessduring their next rest.
features.trigger: When an ally with a shadow token moves to within Melee range of the Huntmaster

name: Umbral Mastiff
description: A large, shadow-drenched skeletal canine with soul- rending claws, always hungry and never satisfied.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: The Mastiff may move from one shadow to another within Close range as if they were adjacent.

features.name: Rend Soul
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success,spend a Fearas the Mastiff bites and tears at the target’s soul, dealing1d10+2direct magic damage. AShadowless target must additionally mark a Stress.

features.name: Pack Protector
features.description: When an ally within Very Close range would take damage,mark a Stressto move the Mastiff into Melee with the ally and take the damage instead.
features.trigger: When an ally within Very Close range would take damage

name: Undead Wyvern
description: A flying drake that has lost most of its scales

features.name: Flying Predator
features.description: While the Wyvern is flying, mark a Stress to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Corrosive Bite
features.description: When the Wyvern makes a successful standard attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can’t mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Wretched Breath
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d4). When the Wyvern first enters the scene, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Wyvern breathes poisonous gas on all targets in front of them up to Close range. All targets in this area must make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail mark 2 Stress and are Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. Targets who succeed mark a Stress. Tier 2 - The First Fallen During the destruction of the Exodus, cataclysmic fire rained down, burning the valleys and crumbling the monuments of men. Some of those who perished became terrible undead, their empty husks still clinging to routine.
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 1d4)

features.name: Inspiration Minis
features.description: Archvillain Games Gravebound Series

name: Untamed Experiment
description: A creature whose body is made through the whims of its creator. Some have the body of lions or the hindquarters of horses, others are goats that breathe fire.

features.name: Mad Creation
features.description: When the Experiment appears, put 8 tokens on this stat block. Spend these tokens to add features to the Experiment when it appears or during its spotlight. These are one time costs.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Experiment can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flying
features.description: The Experiment has wings that can support flight. While Flying, the Experiment gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Ophidian Charm
features.description: When a PC makes an attack against the Experiment within Melee range they must roll a d6. On a 3 or lower, the creature has disadvantage on their next attack roll and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Terrifying Roar
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to roar loudly, intimidating all foes nearby. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are Shaken until they roll with Hope. While Shaken, a target's Hope die is reduced to a d10.

features.name: Double Swipe
features.description: When the Experiment makes a standard attack, mark a Stress to make another against a different target within Melee range.

features.name: Ramming Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to charge through a group within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Experiment's path. Targets the Experiment succeeds against take 2d6+10 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Powerful Kick
features.description: When the Experiment takes Major or greater damage, mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes 1d12+2 physical damage and is knocked back to Far range.
features.trigger: takes Major or greater damage

features.name: Burning Breath
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d4). When the Experiment sees a hostile target, activate the countdown. When it triggers, the Experiment breathes flames at all targets in front of them within a Very Close range. All targets in the area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 1d8+3 magic damage. For each target that marks HP, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: sees hostile target

name: Urstrix
description: A bear with a feathered upper body and the head of a large owl.

features.name: Silent Stalker
features.description: If an Urstrix is Hidden when they make a successful standard attack against a target, they deal 1d20+3 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Heavy Swipe
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Urstrix within Very Close range. Targets the Urstrix succeeds against take 1d10+2 physical damage and are knocked back to Close range. If you instead spend a Fear to use this feature, the damage becomes direct.

features.name: Piercing Screech
features.description: When the Urstrix takes Major or greater damage, spend a Fear to let out a furious hoot. All targets within Close range must mark 1d4 Stress and the Urstrix becomes Hidden until their next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (14) to find them.
features.trigger: takes Major or greater damage

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Urstrix makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: successful attack against PC

name: Velk the Forsaken
description: Velk Dravenmoor, wounded and corrupted by dark powers,

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Velk can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight him.

features.name: Leaping Strike
features.description: Spend a Fear to allow Velk to leap anywhere within Far range and make an attack. On a success, he deals an additional1d12damage.

features.name: Spinning Blade
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range.

features.name: Throw
features.description: Mark a Stressto make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal1d8+3physical damage and the target must make a Strength Reaction Roll (13) or be moved anywhere within Close range.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When Velk makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. adversariesdesignedforstandardcampaignplay.

name: Watchling
description: A levitating mushroom creature with a central eye and a few small tentacles and eyes on stalks protruding from their body.

features.name: Penetrating Gaze
features.description: When a PC uses an Armor Slot against the Watchling's attack, you can mark a Stress to force the target to mark a HP.
features.trigger: When a PC uses an Armor Slot against the Watchling's attack

features.name: Cry for Help
features.description: Countdown (4). The first time the Watchling sees the PCs, start the countdown. When it triggers, 1d4 adversaries of the PCs' Tier appear at Far range. Wight Wights are intelligent undead who possess at least part of a soul. Since returning to life their body doesn’t continue to decompose. Some Wights have the souls of their original body while others are formed from a soul entering into a new body.
features.trigger: Countdown (4)

name: Weaponmaster
description: Un combattant compétent maîtrisant de nombreuses armes.

name: Woad Stalker
description: A spirit that has animated the detritus and plants that cover the forest floor into the shape of an animal. It has a golden glow from within.

features.name: Spirit Form
features.description: The Stalker is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Fall Apart
features.description: The Stalker can mark a Stress to become Hidden until their next attack. Attacks made while the Stalker is Hidden have advantage.

features.name: Reclamate
features.description: Spend a Fear to rebuild parts of the Stalker's animated exterior, clearing 1 HP. The Stalker can mark a Stress to take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.

name: Yoblin
description: A vicious goblin with no sense of self-preservation.

features.name: Bind
features.description: Choose a target with at least 3 Yoblins within Melee range and spotlight these Minions. Make one shared attack roll. On a success, the target is bound in rope. While bound the target moves with the Minions and is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll (13). For each Minion above 3, add a +1 bonus to the escape Difficulty.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Yoblins within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 3 physical damage each. Combine this damage. Gods Gods are extremely powerful beings whose power covers one or more domains. Their consciousness extends across realms, perceiving events involving their followers or otherwise involved with their domain. The pathway to Godhood is a closely guarded secret, known only to a few. There are four steps in total: 1)	Acquire a Divine Spark. This is most commonly achieved through gathering worshippers or being born with it. A Divine Spark unlocks Godly abilities. 2)	Claim a domain. A soul anchor can be created using the Divine Spark. It must be tethered to the heart of a demiplane representing a Godly domain to claim that domain. If another God has this domain in their portfolio, their soul anchor must first be destroyed unless they agree to share the domain. 3)	Achieve immortality. Becoming unable to be killed is required to Ascend. 4)	Complete a ritual called the Rite of Ascension. In this final step, a large source of energy is channeled into the new God. They must survive taking this magical damage to Ascend, after which they gain divine powers. Once Ascended, the power of a God depends on size and fervor of worship, beginning with epic mortal powers (Tier 1), increasing to region-affecting powers (Tier 2) and then world-affecting powers (Tier 3). Examples for different domains are provided in the God Powers table opposite. name: Young Dryad
description: Un jeune esprit de la nature sous forme humanoïde.

name: Young Earth Drake
description: A wingless dragon-like creature whose scales resemble dirt clods that occasionally crackle with static electricity.

features.name: Tough Hide
features.description: When the Drake takes physical damage, reduce it by 3.

features.name: Chomp
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, spend a Fear to deal an additional 1d12 physical damage and the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Stomp
features.description: Spend a Fear to slam the ground with the Drake's powerful legs, creating a shockwave. All targets within Close range must make a Strength or Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 1d10 magic damage and are Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. On a success they take half damage.

features.name: Dug In
features.description: If the Drake is hit by an attack or ability that would force it to move, you can mark 2 Stress to remain in position.
features.trigger: hit by attack/ability that forces movement

name: Zombie Pack
description: Un groupe titubant de morts-vivants, dangereux en nombre.

features.name: Horde (1d6+1)
features.description: Quand la Horde de Zombies a coché la moitié ou plus de ses PV, son attaque standard inflige 1d6+1 dégâts physiques à la place.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: Quand une créature inflige des dégâts à la Horde de Zombies, faites une attaque standard contre cette créature.
features.trigger: Quand une créature inflige des dégâts à la horde de zombies
